mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances or t-statistic for return difference is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in greater than 50% of cases or interaction term coefficient turns negative in OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 3% annually or 20-day returns are negative in greater than 55% of rising INDPRO periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust employment growth that supports aggregate demand and corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict returns with Rank IC greater than 0.01 over 5-year rolling windows or positive return frequency falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of threshold-crossing events or inflation spikes fail to precede market declines.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures where consumption outpaces GDP signals strong private sector momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Consumption-GDP divergence yields negative returns in greater than 50% of cases or interaction effect vanishes in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during periods of elevated capacity utilization indicates supply constraints and impending output gaps, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day returns remain positive in greater than 60% of declining INDPRO periods or signal fails to predict negative earnings revisions.
